result: All five known A385316 terms reproduce with correct representation counts. The verifier is now validated against the complete known table. The verifier also correctly kills domain-bad (49) and predicate-violating (185527 with target_rep=6) fixtures. No search, enumeration, or a(6) discovery was performed — the bounded exact search remains deferred per contract. The quarantine from the prior run (missing domain/predicate lines under Verified) is resolved: both exact lines are present in this receipt.
next_gate: Run one bounded exact-search sweep at cutoff C up to 1e12 (the ~10GB budget from the frontier_atlas entry), using the verified complete-prime-set multiplicity algorithm, to either find a(6) or certify a(6) > 499243435237 (the published bound) and potentially raise it. Limit output to ≤4000 characters. The sweep should fit in <1GB memory with primes ≤ C^(1/3) and O(|P|²) pairwise enumeration.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training. All work confined to the research session artifact directory. The verifier artifact is a no-argument Python replay entrypoint that exits zero with complete kill-fixture summaries.
